response.justification: This candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with strong craftsmanship credentials, but his profile diverges sharply from this role's core requirements. His C++ experience amounts to a seven-month stint at National Instruments ending in 2015 plus a bachelor-thesis project, and C++ does not even appear in his skills list; the last decade has been TypeScript/Node.js web development and DLang services. He shows no exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL secure development practices, MQTT, message brokers at the architectural level, or any of the listed industrial protocols. His low-latency systems work and Node/React familiarity are genuine assets, but they do not offset the absence of the role's central competency: modern, hands-on C++/STL development in an industrial context.
strengths: Nearly two decades of broad engineering experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Proven high-performance systems work (asynchronous I/O, fibers, caching for millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong quality culture: TDD, open-source test tooling author, mentoring, workshops, and technical talks
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014–2015) and C++ is absent from his current skills list
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or fieldbus/OT protocol experience anywhere in the history
Recent decade is web/full-stack TypeScript and DLang work, far from the multi-threaded C++/STL platform engineering this role centers on
